abstract.para: This topic covers how to install and configure &kdump;.

topic.para: This command downloads the following packages:

listitem.para: To store the vmcore file remotely:
Install the cifs-utils package to store on Samba stores.
Install the lftp package to store on FTP / SFTP servers.
Install the nfs-client package to store on NFS servers.
Install the openssh-clients package to store over SSH.
The mailx utility to enable sending notifications when a crash dump is saved.

step.para: Configure and enable the &kdump; memory reservation:

To use &kdump;, a specific amount of system memory (RAM)—often referred to as crashkernel memory must be permanently reserved at boot time for the capture kernel and initramfs. Add crashkernel= kernel to the production kernel's command-line arguments.
&kdump; includes automated tools to calculate exactly how much memory needs to be reserved:
Enable and immediately start the &kdump; service:

When the systemctl command triggers the main kdump.service, it automatically calls a helper service named kdump-commandline.service before attempting to load the kernel. This helper service verifies if the current kernel command line contains the expected crashkernel= options. If they are missing, automatically updates the boot loader configuration to apply the settings on the next boot sequence.
Once booted, the production kernel reserves the designated crashkernel memory, allowing the &kdump; service to load successfully and remain active to capture system panics.
Ensure the specialized crash dump initial ramdisk (initramfs) is successfully built so the capture kernel has the necessary drivers to load upon a crash.
Check the output of the configuration service to ensure it successfully modified the boot loader:
step.screen: Started Check and update kdump options on the kernel command line. Kdump expects these crashkernel= values on the kernel command line: crashkernel=72M,low crashkernel=443M,high No crashkernel= found in /proc/cmdline. Kdump can't be started. Updated the command line options in the bootloader. Changes will take effect on next boot.

step.para: If the logs confirm the boot loader was updated, reboot the machine to apply the allocated memory changes and initialize &kdump;:
